channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Quantum Computing Without the PhD: A Developer’s Guide (Part 2)
item.description: Welcome back to Quantum For Devs. If you read Part 1, you know that we absolutely refuse to use the “spinning coin” analogy to explain quantum computing here. If not, you can read by clicking this...

item.title: Everyone Talks About ChatGPT — But AI's Future Is Actually in Embedded Devices
item.description: Cloud AI gets the headlines, but the next wave of intelligence is running on microcontrollers with kilobytes of RAM. Ask most people where AI “lives,” and they'll point at a data center. ChatGPT,...

item.title: Continuous Learning Won't Come From the Weights
item.description: The one gap everyone agrees on In a rare investor briefing, DeepSeek's Liang Wenfeng was asked what's actually missing on the road to AGI. His answer wasn't a bigger model, or video, or another...

item.title: Building an Operating System In Rust Part 2
item.description: In Part 1 we built the foundation of our operating system using Rust, dove deep into murky low level waters and broke free from the shackles of stdlib while still keeping the compiler happy. Now...

item.title: Debugging a Broken Ingress Setup: A Step-by-Step Walkthrough
item.description: This post documents a real debugging session that started with a simple symptom — example.com wouldn't load — and ended up uncovering several separate, unrelated problems layered on top of each...

item.title: Some Kids Will Never Think AI Is Cool
item.description: “I think it should stand for artificial idiot,” one 9-year-old says. Here’s why kids of all ages are calling AI “disgusting” and “creepy.”

item.title: Ich habe einen echten Speedtest in Vanilla JS gebaut (mit Cloudflare API)
item.description: Ich habe einen echten Speedtest in Vanilla JS gebaut (mit Cloudflare API) Kein npm install. Kein React. Kein 200-MB-node_modules-Ordner. Nur HTML, CSS und ~250 Zeilen JavaScript, die deine echte...

item.title: Building an Operating System In Rust Part 1
item.description: Building an operating system is a project I have had my eyes set on ever since I discovered free will in the realm of programming. Years ago, I did a reasonable amount of research, paying extra...

item.title: Understanding FilesystemBackend in LangChain's Deep Agents
item.description: In a previous example, we used StateBackend, where files belonged to a specific LangGraph conversation thread. This time, we are using something different: FilesystemBackend lets a Deep Agent read...

item.title: I Asked My AI "Who Are You" — It Cost 42,000 Tokens to Answer
item.description: I typed a 19-character question into Claude Code: who are you? Then I opened the network logs of what my machine actually sent over the wire. I recommend you never do this. It ruins something in...

item.title: Show HN: A static, client-side tarot calculator for Brazilian Portuguese
item.description: Article URL: https://meuarcanopessoal.net/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=49033173 Points: 5 # Comments: 1

item.title: Microsoft pressures LG into killing unwanted McAfee ads
item.description: Microsoft has intervened to stop Windows 11 users with LG monitors from being bombarded with annoying McAfee trial pop-ups. In response to complaints about the LG bloatware, Microsoft's Windows...

item.title: NodeBB Patches Eight AI-Found Flaws Exposing Admin Access and Private Chats
item.description: Eight security flaws in NodeBB went public on Wednesday, along with the code to exploit them. Aikido Security rates all eight as high severity and says its AI pentest agents found them in a...

item.title: Kimi K3 Agents Found Redis Zero-Days and Built RCE Exploit, Researchers Say
item.description: Redis shipped seven security releases on July 23 after researchers published authenticated RCE PoCs for stock Redis 6.2.22, 7.4.9, 8.6.4, and 8.8.0. All four chains require RESTORE. The Streams...

item.title: Fake Notepad++ Plugin Delivers MATCHBOIL.V2 in UAC-0099 Attacks
item.description: The Computer Emergency Response Team of Ukraine (CERT-UA) has warned of a new campaign that involves the use of a malicious program that's dressed up as a Notepad++ plugin to compromise Windows...

item.title: How AI guardrails are impeding the work of offensive cybersecurity researchers
item.description: We spoke with several cybersecurity researchers, who look for unknown vulnerabilities and develop tools to exploit them, about how OpenAI’s and Anthropic’s guardrails affect their work.

item.title: Alexa Plus is getting an AI update to handle more complicated instructions
item.description: Amazon is launching an update to its Alexa Plus assistant that will allow it to connect to smart home devices in new ways. With the update, which is currently in preview, Alexa Plus can link up...

item.title: The Echo Show 21 is a great smart home hub that’s $80 off
item.description: Split between buying a smart calendar, a kitchen TV, a smart home hub, and a smart display? Amazon’s Echo Show 21 is all of those things in one, with a huge 21-inch screen. You can use it to...

item.title: AMD takes on Nvidia with its Helios AI rack-scale system
item.description: AMD is challenging its chipmaker rival with a new rack-scale system that will start shipping to customers later this year.

item.title: FCC Chairman Brendan Carr’s war on the First Amendment
item.description: As the chairman of the Federal Communications Commission, Brendan Carr has authority over the nation’s TV, radio, and internet. But since Donald Trump was elected to his second term, Carr has...

item.title: Anthropic updates Claude voice mode with more capable models
item.description: Claude's new voice model will let you reschedule your meeting or draft an email.

item.title: Claude’s voice mode is now available for Opus and Sonnet
item.description: Until now, voice mode has only been available on Claude Haiku, Anthropic's faster but less powerful model. Now the company is making its Opus and Sonnet models available in voice mode, and...

item.title: Meta’s New Feel-Good AI Ad Uses a Song About the World Ending
item.description: The clip features the David Bowie track “Five Years,” which includes lyrics such as “Earth was really dying (dying).”

item.title: AegisAI, founded by former Google security execs, lands $36M to stop AI-driven spear phishing
item.description: AegisAI co-founders developed AI agents that quickly analyze each message as a human would, paying attention to small anomalies that even the most elaborate checklist wouldn’t catch.

item.title: Russian Espionage Group Exploited Zimbra Zero-Day to Steal Mail and 2FA Codes
item.description: A Russian state-supported espionage group spent months reading Western mailboxes through a then-unknown flaw in Zimbra's webmail client. The payload goes after the last 90 days of email, the...

item.title: Patreon is laying off 20 percent of workers
item.description: Patreon is laying off 20 percent of its workers, or around 93 employees, as reported earlier by 404 Media. In a memo to employees, Patreon CEO Jack Conte writes that the company isn't making these...

item.title: Corsair’s PC case with a panoramic glass design is $70 off
item.description: On the hunt for a PC case that’s loaded with features and has a unique look? Amazon, Best Buy, and Corsair are selling the Corsair Frame 4500X RS for $119.99, a $70 discount from the usual price....

item.title: Tesla’s robotaxi promises are clashing with reality
item.description: In an earnings call yesterday, Tesla CEO Elon Musk did his best to paint a positive portrait of the company's robotaxi program. New cities are being added, more miles are being driven, and more...

item.title: Runway launches AI model router as generative media gets crowded
item.description: The Media Router is a tool that automatically selects the best image, video, or audio generation model for a request based on whether a developer prioritizes quality, speed or cost.

item.title: OpenAI makes ChatGPT Health available to all US users
item.description: Users can also integrate their personal data from services like Apple Health, Function, and MyFitnessPal.
